CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION total_loans()
    RETURNS TRIGGER 
    AS $$ 
    BEGIN 
        -- Update the new account's summary
        UPDATE loan_summary 
        SET total_loan_count = (
            SELECT COUNT(*) 
            FROM loan 
            WHERE loan.account_id = NEW.account_id 
            AND status = 'A'
        ) 
        WHERE account_id = NEW.account_id;
        
        -- If account_id changed, update the old account's summary too
        IF OLD.account_id != NEW.account_id THEN
            UPDATE loan_summary 
            SET total_loan_count = (
                SELECT COUNT(*) 
                FROM loan 
                WHERE loan.account_id = OLD.account_id 
                AND status = 'A'
            ) 
            WHERE account_id = OLD.account_id;
        END IF;
        
        RETURN NEW; 
    END; 
    $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql

WITH user_groups AS (
  -- First, find all connected users through shared refs
  SELECT DISTINCT
    u1.id as user_id,
    COALESCE(u2.ref1, u1.ref1, u2.ref2, u1.ref2) as group_ref
  FROM users u1
  LEFT JOIN users u2 ON u1.ref1 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref1 = u2.ref2 
                     OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref2
),
merged_users AS (
  -- Group users by their shared reference
  SELECT 
    MIN(group_ref) as group_id,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT user_id) as ids
  FROM user_groups
  GROUP BY group_ref
),
asset_ownership AS (
  -- Get assets with their owners, including orphaned assets
  SELECT 
    a.id as asset_id,
    a.owner,
    u.id as user_id
  FROM assets a
  LEFT JOIN users u ON a.owner = u.ref1 OR a.owner = u.ref2
)
SELECT 
  COALESCE(mu.ids, ARRAY[]::integer[]) as ids,
  ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.username) FILTER (WHERE u.username IS NOT NULL) as usernames,
  ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.ref1) FILTER (WHERE u.ref1 IS NOT NULL) as refs1,
  ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT COALESCE(u.ref2, ao.owner)) as refs2,
  COUNT(DISTINCT ao.asset_id) as asset_count,
  ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ao.asset_id ORDER BY ao.asset_id) as asset_ids
FROM asset_ownership ao
LEFT JOIN merged_users mu ON ao.user_id = ANY(mu.ids)
LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = ANY(COALESCE(mu.ids, ARRAY[ao.user_id]))
GROUP BY mu.group_id, ao.owner, mu.ids
ORDER BY MIN(ao.asset_id)

(
  SELECT
    account.account_id,
    account.frequency,
    l.loan_id,
    l.date AS loan_date,
    0 AS priority
  FROM account
  LEFT JOIN loan l
    ON account.account_id = l.account_id
  WHERE account.district_id = '18'
    AND l.date >= (NOW() - INTERVAL '48 hours')
  ORDER BY l.date DESC NULLS LAST
  LIMIT 6
)
UNION
(
  SELECT
    account.account_id,
    account.frequency,
    l.loan_id,
    l.date AS loan_date,
    1 AS priority
  FROM account
  LEFT JOIN loan l
    ON account.account_id = l.account_id
  WHERE account.district_id = '18'
    AND account.account_id NOT IN (
      SELECT account.account_id
      FROM account
      LEFT JOIN loan l ON account.account_id = l.account_id
      WHERE account.district_id = '18'
        AND l.date >= (NOW() - INTERVAL '48 hours')
      LIMIT 6
    )
  ORDER BY account.date DESC
)
ORDER BY priority, loan_date DESC NULLS LAST

WITH distinct_dates AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT releasedate
    FROM sets_releaseinfo
    WHERE releasedate IS NOT NULL
),
grouped_dates AS (
    SELECT 
        releasedate,
        releasedate - (ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY releasedate) || ' days')::INTERVAL AS grp
    FROM distinct_dates
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS count,
    MIN(releasedate) AS date_min,
    MAX(releasedate) AS date_max
FROM grouped_dates
GROUP BY grp
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY count DESC, date_min

WITH RECURSIVE path_cte AS (
  -- Base case: Start from cards that have no previous card (head of chains)
  SELECT 
    c.id, 
    c.nextCardId, 
    c.id::TEXT AS Path,
    c.id AS start_id
  FROM collection c
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM collection c2 
    WHERE c2.nextCardId = c.id
  )
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive case: Follow the nextCardId chain forward
  SELECT 
    c.id, 
    c.nextCardId, 
    cte.Path || ' --> ' || c.id::TEXT,
    cte.start_id
  FROM collection c
  JOIN path_cte cte ON cte.nextCardId = c.id
)
SELECT Path
FROM path_cte
WHERE nextCardId IS NULL  -- Only show complete paths (ending at tail nodes)
ORDER BY start_id

-- Create the custom function
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION what_quarter_is(date_in DATE, start_month INTEGER DEFAULT 1)
RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
BEGIN
    RETURN FLOOR(
        (EXTRACT(MONTH FROM date_in) - start_month + 12) % 12 / 3.0
    )::INTEGER + 1;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql IMMUTABLE;

-- Example usage with your date
SELECT what_quarter_is('2001-02-16'::DATE, 4) AS quarter;

-- Or for the trans table with April as Q1 start:
SELECT 
    trans_id,
    date,
    what_quarter_is(date, 4) AS fiscal_quarter,
    amount
FROM trans
ORDER BY date
LIMIT 10

SELECT 
    base_table.relname as base_table,
    base_attr.attname as base_col,
    ref_table.relname as referencing_table,
    ref_attr.attname as referencing_col,
    pg_get_constraintdef(c.oid) as constraint_sql
FROM pg_constraint c
JOIN pg_class base_table ON c.confrelid = base_table.oid
JOIN pg_class ref_table ON c.conrelid = ref_table.oid
JOIN LATERAL unnest(c.confkey, c.conkey) WITH ORDINALITY AS keys(base_attnum, ref_attnum, ord) ON true
JOIN pg_attribute base_attr ON base_attr.attrelid = c.confrelid AND base_attr.attnum = keys.base_attnum
JOIN pg_attribute ref_attr ON ref_attr.attrelid = c.conrelid AND ref_attr.attnum = keys.ref_attnum
WHERE base_table.relname = 'users'
  AND c.contype = 'f'
  AND c.confrelid != c.conrelid
ORDER BY referencing_table, referencing_col

WITH ranked_banks AS (
    SELECT 
        trans_id,
        account_id,
        date,
        bank,
        CASE 
            WHEN bank IS NOT NULL THEN 
                ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY account_id ORDER BY date, trans_id)
        END AS bank_group
    FROM trans
),
filled_banks AS (
    SELECT 
        trans_id,
        account_id,
        date,
        bank,
        MAX(bank_group) OVER (
            PARTITION BY account_id 
            ORDER BY date, trans_id 
            ROWS BETWEEN CURRENT ROW AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
        ) AS next_bank_group
    FROM ranked_banks
)
SELECT 
    fb.trans_id,
    fb.account_id,
    fb.date,
    fb.bank,
    rb.bank AS next_bank
FROM filled_banks fb
LEFT JOIN ranked_banks rb 
    ON fb.account_id = rb.account_id 
    AND fb.next_bank_group = rb.bank_group

WITH one AS (
    SELECT date_trunc('year', date) as timeOne, COUNT(*) as trans_count
    FROM trans
    GROUP BY date_trunc('year', date)
),
two AS (
    SELECT date_trunc('year', date) as timeTwo, COUNT(*) as loan_count
    FROM loan
    GROUP BY date_trunc('year', date)
)
SELECT 
    COALESCE(timeOne, timeTwo) as year,
    COALESCE(trans_count, 0) + COALESCE(loan_count, 0) as count
FROM one
FULL OUTER JOIN two ON timeOne = timeTwo
ORDER BY year

WITH monthly_purchases AS (
    -- Get distinct user-month combinations
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        user_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_DATE(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD')) AS purchase_month
    FROM purchase
),
streak_groups AS (
    -- Identify streak groups using the difference between row number and month sequence
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        purchase_month,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', purchase_month) - 
            (ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY purchase_month) || ' months')::INTERVAL AS streak_group
    FROM monthly_purchases
),
streak_lengths AS (
    -- Count the length of each streak for each user
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        streak_group,
        COUNT(*) AS streak_length
    FROM streak_groups
    GROUP BY user_id, streak_group
),
longest_streaks AS (
    -- Get the longest streak for each user
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        MAX(streak_length) AS longest_streak
    FROM streak_lengths
    GROUP BY user_id
)
-- Count how many users have each longest streak length
SELECT 
    longest_streak AS streak_length,
    COUNT(*) AS num_users
FROM longest_streaks
GROUP BY longest_streak
ORDER BY streak_length

SELECT 
    COUNT(S.id) as race_count,
    SUM(COUNT(S.id)) OVER (PARTITION BY A.alignment) as alignment_total,
    A.alignment,
    R.race
FROM superhero S
INNER JOIN alignment A ON S.alignment_id = A.id
INNER JOIN race R ON S.race_id = R.id
GROUP BY A.alignment, R.race

SELECT 
    driverId, 
    points, 
    lead(driverId) OVER (ORDER BY points ASC) AS "lead(driverId) with order", 
    array_agg(driverId) OVER (ORDER BY points ASC ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING) AS "array_agg(driverId) with order - full window", 
    array_agg(driverId) OVER (ORDER BY points ASC) AS "array_agg(driverId) with order - cumulative", 
    lead(driverId) OVER () AS "lead(driverId) without order", 
    array_agg(driverId) OVER () AS "array_agg(driverId) without order" 
FROM results 
WHERE raceId = 19 
ORDER BY driverId ASC

SELECT 
    JSON_OBJECT_AGG(
        label,
        molecules
    ) AS result
FROM (
    SELECT 
        m.label,
        JSON_AGG(
            JSON_BUILD_OBJECT(
                'molecule_id', m.molecule_id,
                'atoms', (
                    SELECT JSON_AGG(a.atom_id)
                    FROM atom a
                    WHERE a.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
                ),
                'bonds', (
                    SELECT JSON_AGG(b.bond_id)
                    FROM bond b
                    WHERE b.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
                )
            )
        ) AS molecules
    FROM molecule m
    GROUP BY m.label
) subquery

        THEN 1 
        ELSE 0 
    END AS c_atoms
FROM atom 
GROUP BY molecule_id

SELECT 
    home_team_api_id,

    AVG(away_team_goal) as avg_away_goals, 
    SUM(home_team_goal) as total_home_goals, 
    SUM(away_team_goal) as total_away_goals, 
    MAX(home_team_goal) as max_home_goals, 
    MIN(home_team_goal) as min_home_goals,

FROM match 
GROUP BY home_team_api_id, EXTRACT(HOUR FROM date::timestamp)

WITH sign_changes AS (
  SELECT 
    transaction_date,
    club_name,
    amount,
    sign(amount) AS amount_sign,
    SUM(CASE 
      WHEN sign(amount) != LAG(sign(amount), 1, sign(amount)) OVER (PARTITION BY club_name ORDER BY transaction_date) 
      THEN 1 
      ELSE 0 
    END) OVER (PARTITION BY club_name ORDER BY transaction_date) AS sign_group
  FROM club_transactions
),
grouped_transactions AS (
  SELECT 
    MIN(transaction_date) AS date,
    club_name,
    SUM(amount) AS total_amount
  FROM sign_changes
  GROUP BY club_name, sign_group, amount_sign
  ORDER BY club_name, MIN(transaction_date)
)
SELECT date, club_name, total_amount
FROM grouped_transactions
ORDER BY club_name, date

SELECT json_agg(
    json_build_object(
        'id_module', p.id_module,
        'desc_module', p.desc_module,
        'module_code', p.module_code,
        'name_module', p.name_module,
        'id_parent_module', p.id_parent_module,
        'children', COALESCE(
            (
                SELECT json_agg(
                    json_build_object(
                        'id_module', c.id_module,
                        'desc_module', c.desc_module,
                        'module_code', c.module_code,
                        'name_module', c.name_module,
                        'id_parent_module', c.id_parent_module
                    )
                )
                FROM modules c
                WHERE c.id_parent_module = p.id_module
            ),
            '[]'::json
        )
    )
)
FROM modules p
WHERE p.id_parent_module IS NULL

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.generate_series_monthly(a date, b date) 
RETURNS SETOF date 
LANGUAGE SQL 
IMMUTABLE 
PARALLEL SAFE 
ROWS 12 
AS $function$ 
  SELECT generate_series(
    date_trunc('month', a)::date, 
    date_trunc('month', b)::date, 
    '1 month'::interval
  )::date
$function$;

WITH numbered_matches AS (
    SELECT 
        m.id,
        m.date,
        m.home_team_goal,
        m.away_team_goal,
        CASE WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS invoiced,
        SUM(CASE WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
            OVER (ORDER BY m.id, m.date) AS draw_group
    FROM match AS m
),
accumulated AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        date,
        invoiced,
        draw_group,
        SUM(home_team_goal + away_team_goal)
            OVER (PARTITION BY draw_group ORDER BY id, date) AS amount
    FROM numbered_matches
)
SELECT 
    id,
    date,
    invoiced,
    amount
FROM accumulated
WHERE invoiced = 1
ORDER BY id, date

WITH LastTransactionPerStation AS (
  -- Get the last transaction for each customer at each gas station
  SELECT
    *,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid 
      ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC, transactionid DESC
    ) AS rn
  FROM transactions_1k
),
FilteredTransactions AS (
  -- Keep only the last transaction per customer per gas station
  SELECT *
  FROM LastTransactionPerStation
  WHERE rn = 1
),
CustomerIssueAnalysis AS (
  -- Determine if customer has any amount < 10 issues and find the first one chronologically
  SELECT
    customerid,
    gasstationid,
    transactionid,
    amount,
    date,
    time,
    CASE 
      WHEN amount < 10 THEN 1 
      ELSE 0 
    END AS has_issue,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY customerid 
      ORDER BY 
        CASE WHEN amount < 10 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,  -- Issues first
        date ASC, 
        time ASC, 
        transactionid ASC
    ) AS issue_rank,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY customerid 
      ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC, transactionid DESC
    ) AS last_rank
  FROM FilteredTransactions
),
CustomerDecision AS (
  -- Decide which gas station to show per customer
  SELECT
    customerid,
    MAX(CASE WHEN has_issue = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_any_issue
  FROM CustomerIssueAnalysis
  GROUP BY customerid
)
SELECT
  cia.customerid,
  cia.transactionid,
  cia.gasstationid,
  cia.amount
FROM CustomerIssueAnalysis cia
JOIN CustomerDecision cd ON cia.customerid = cd.customerid
WHERE
  -- If customer has issues, show the first issue occurrence
  (cd.has_any_issue = 1 AND cia.has_issue = 1 AND cia.issue_rank = 1)
  OR
  -- If customer has no issues, show the last transaction
  (cd.has_any_issue = 0 AND cia.last_rank = 1)
ORDER BY cia.customerid, cia.gasstationid

WITH search_with_next_login AS (
  SELECT 
    s.user_id,
    s.action_time AS search_time,
    MIN(l.action_time) AS next_login_time
  FROM user_actions s
  LEFT JOIN user_actions l 
    ON s.user_id = l.user_id 
    AND l.action = 'LOGIN' 
    AND l.action_time > s.action_time
  WHERE s.action = 'SEARCH'
  GROUP BY s.user_id, s.action_time
)
SELECT 
  swl.user_id,
  COUNT(*) AS abandoned_search_count
FROM search_with_next_login swl
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 
  FROM user_actions b
  WHERE b.user_id = swl.user_id
    AND b.action = 'BUY'
    AND b.action_time > swl.search_time
    AND (swl.next_login_time IS NULL OR b.action_time < swl.next_login_time)
)
GROUP BY swl.user_id

SELECT 
    player_id,
    MAX(CASE WHEN stat_key = 'goals' THEN stat_value END) AS goals,
    MAX(CASE WHEN stat_key = 'assists' THEN stat_value END) AS assists,
    MAX(CASE WHEN stat_key = 'yellow_cards' THEN stat_value END) AS yellow_cards,
    MAX(CASE WHEN stat_key = 'red_cards' THEN stat_value END) AS red_cards,
    MAX(CASE WHEN stat_key = 'shots' THEN stat_value END) AS shots,
    MAX(CASE WHEN stat_key = 'passes' THEN stat_value END) AS passes
FROM (
    SELECT 
        player_id,
        TRIM(split_part(stats_keys, ',', n)) AS stat_key,
        TRIM(split_part(stats_values, ',', n)) AS stat_value
    FROM player_stats
    CROSS JOIN (
        SELECT 1 AS n UNION ALL
        SELECT 2 UNION ALL
        SELECT 3 UNION ALL
        SELECT 4 UNION ALL
        SELECT 5 UNION ALL
        SELECT 6
    ) AS numbers
    WHERE split_part(stats_keys, ',', n) != ''
) AS unpivoted
GROUP BY player_id
ORDER BY player_id

        - EXTRACT(MONTH FROM begin_date)
        - CASE WHEN EXTRACT(DAY FROM begin_date) > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
        - CASE WHEN end_date < (DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date) + INTERVAL '1 month - 1 day')::date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
    ) AS full_calendar_months

WITH pivoted_changes AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        updated_at,
        MAX(CASE WHEN column_id = 'height' THEN value END) as height_change,
        MAX(CASE WHEN column_id = 'weight' THEN value END) as weight_change
    FROM player_stats_changes
    GROUP BY entity_id, updated_at
)
SELECT 
    entity_id,
    COALESCE(
        height_change,
        LAST_VALUE(height_change IGNORE NULLS) 
            OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY updated_at 
                  ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)
    ) as height,
    COALESCE(
        weight_change,
        LAST_VALUE(weight_change IGNORE NULLS) 
            OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY updated_at 
                  ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)
    ) as weight,
    updated_at
FROM pivoted_changes
ORDER BY entity_id, updated_at

WITH qr1(item, subgroup) AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup1 
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
), 
qr2(item, subgroup) AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup2 
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
),
combined AS (
    SELECT qr1.item, qr1.subgroup as subgroup1, qr2.subgroup as subgroup2 
    FROM qr1 
    FULL OUTER JOIN qr2 ON qr1.item = qr2.item
),
-- Create edges between items that share subgroups
edges AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT c1.item as item1, c2.item as item2
    FROM combined c1
    JOIN combined c2 ON (c1.subgroup1 = c2.subgroup1 OR c1.subgroup2 = c2.subgroup2)
    WHERE c1.item < c2.item
),
-- Find connected components using recursive CTE
components AS (
    SELECT item, item as group_id
    FROM combined
    
    UNION
    
    SELECT c.item, LEAST(c.group_id, e.item2) as group_id
    FROM components c
    JOIN edges e ON c.item = e.item1
    
    UNION
    
    SELECT c.item, LEAST(c.group_id, e.item1) as group_id
    FROM components c
    JOIN edges e ON c.item = e.item2
)
SELECT 
    c.item, 
    c.subgroup1, 
    c.subgroup2,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY MIN(comp.group_id)) as group_id
FROM combined c
JOIN components comp ON c.item = comp.item
GROUP BY c.item, c.subgroup1, c.subgroup2
ORDER BY group_id, c.item

    AND (u.utm_medium IS NULL OR r.utm_medium = u.utm_medium)
    AND (u.utm_content IS NULL OR r.utm_content = u.utm_content)
    AND (u.utm_term IS NULL OR r.utm_term = u.utm_term)
GROUP BY

SELECT
    c.id,
    c.name,
    c.text,
    COALESCE(tokens.token_array, ARRAY[]::text[]) AS bracketed_tokens,
    COALESCE(tokens.token_count, 0) AS token_count,
    COALESCE(tokens.tokens_concatenated, '') AS tokens_as_string
FROM cards c
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT
        ARRAY_AGG(m[1] ORDER BY ord) AS token_array,
        COUNT(*) AS token_count,
        STRING_AGG(m[1], ' ' ORDER BY ord) AS tokens_concatenated
    FROM REGEXP_MATCHES(c.text, '(\{\{[^}]+\}\})', 'g') WITH ORDINALITY AS matches(m, ord)
) tokens ON true
WHERE c.text IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY c.id

-- Create the state transition function for forward fill
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION GapFillInternal(s anyelement, v anyelement) 
RETURNS anyelement AS $$ 
BEGIN 
    IF v IS NOT NULL THEN 
        RETURN v;
    ELSIF s IS NOT NULL THEN 
        RETURN s;
    ELSE 
        RETURN NULL;
    END IF;
END; 
$$ LANGUAGE PLPGSQL;

-- Create the aggregate function
CREATE AGGREGATE GapFill(anyelement) (
    SFUNC = GapFillInternal, 
    STYPE = anyelement
);

-- Query to perform forward fill on the example table
SELECT 
    row_num,
    id, 
    str,
    val,
    GapFill(val) OVER (ORDER BY row_num) AS val_filled
FROM example
ORDER BY row_num;

-- Query to perform forward fill on superhero table
SELECT 
    id, 
    publisher_id, 
    height_cm, 
    weight_kg, 
    GapFill(height_cm) OVER (PARTITION BY publisher_id ORDER BY id) AS height_cm_filled, 
    GapFill(weight_kg) OVER (PARTITION BY publisher_id ORDER BY id) AS weight_kg_filled 
FROM superhero
ORDER BY publisher_id, id

SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp = DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 day') THEN amount ELSE NULL END) AS yesterday_revenue,
    ROUND(
        (SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp = DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 day') THEN amount ELSE NULL END) - 
         SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp = DATE('2022-01-27', '-8 days') THEN amount ELSE NULL END)) / 
        CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp = DATE('2022-01-27', '-8 days') THEN amount ELSE NULL END) AS REAL), 
        2
    ) AS pct_change_week_ago,
    SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp >= DATE('2022-01-27', 'start of month') 
             AND timestamp <= DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 day') THEN amount ELSE NULL END) AS mtd,
    ROUND(
        (SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp >= DATE('2022-01-27', 'start of month') 
                  AND timestamp <= DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 day') THEN amount ELSE NULL END) - 
         SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp >= DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 month', 'start of month') 
                  AND timestamp <= DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 month', 'start of month', '+' || (CAST(strftime('%d', DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 day')) AS INTEGER) - 1) || ' days') 
                  THEN amount ELSE NULL END)) / 
        CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp >= DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 month', 'start of month') 
                     AND timestamp <= DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 month', 'start of month', '+' || (CAST(strftime('%d', DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 day')) AS INTEGER) - 1) || ' days') 
                     THEN amount ELSE NULL END) AS REAL), 
        2
    ) AS pct_change_month_prior

WITH parent_posts AS (
  SELECT 
    p1.id as parent_id,
    p1.acceptedanswerid as child_id,
    p1.score as parent_score,
    p2.score as child_score
  FROM posts p1
  LEFT JOIN posts p2 ON p1.acceptedanswerid = p2.id
  WHERE p1.acceptedanswerid IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT DISTINCT p.id, p.acceptedanswerid, p.posttypeid, p.score
FROM posts p
WHERE 
  -- Include parent posts with score >= 20
  (p.id IN (SELECT parent_id FROM parent_posts WHERE parent_score >= 20))
  OR
  -- Include child posts where parent score < 20 but child score >= 20
  (p.id IN (SELECT child_id FROM parent_posts WHERE parent_score < 20 AND child_score >= 20))
  OR
  -- Include posts with score >= 20 that are not involved in any parent-child relationship
  (p.score >= 20 
   AND p.id NOT IN (SELECT parent_id FROM parent_posts WHERE parent_id IS NOT NULL)
   AND p.id NOT IN (SELECT child_id FROM parent_posts WHERE child_id IS NOT NULL))
ORDER BY p.id

WITH card_activity AS (
    SELECT 
        to_date(fe.start_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD') AS start_date,
        to_date(fe.end_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD') AS end_date,
        dp.set_name,
        dp.set_code
    FROM fact_collection fe
    INNER JOIN dim_set dp ON fe.set_key = dp.set_key
),
month_series AS (
    SELECT generate_series(
        (SELECT MIN(to_date(start_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD')) FROM fact_collection),
        CURRENT_DATE,
        INTERVAL '1 month'
    ) AS month_date
)
SELECT 
    date_trunc('month', ms.month_date) AS month,
    ca.set_name,
    COUNT(*) AS existing_cards
FROM month_series ms
CROSS JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT set_name, set_code FROM card_activity) AS sets
LEFT JOIN card_activity ca 
    ON ca.set_name = sets.set_name
    AND ca.start_date <= ms.month_date
    AND (ca.end_date IS NULL OR ca.end_date >= date_trunc('month', ms.month_date))
GROUP BY date_trunc('month', ms.month_date), ca.set_name
ORDER BY month, ca.set_name

WITH ordered_missions AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        mission_start,
        mission_end,
        LAG(mission_end) OVER (PARTITION BY hero_id ORDER BY mission_start) AS prev_mission_end
    FROM superhero_missions
),
mission_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        mission_start,
        mission_end,
        SUM(CASE WHEN mission_start = prev_mission_end THEN 0 ELSE 1 END) 
            OVER (PARTITION BY hero_id ORDER BY mission_start) AS group_id
    FROM ordered_missions
)
SELECT 
    hero_id,
    MIN(mission_start) AS span_start,
    MAX(mission_end) AS span_end
FROM mission_groups
GROUP BY hero_id, group_id
ORDER BY hero_id, span_start

        (3, 3, 75, 75, 70, NULL, 75, 75),

), 
score_check AS (

            WHEN (year_1 IS NULL OR year_2 IS NULL OR year_3 IS NULL OR year_4 IS NULL OR year_5 IS NULL) THEN FALSE
            WHEN (year_1 <> max_score OR year_2 <> max_score OR year_3 <> max_score OR year_4 <> max_score OR year_5 <> max_score) THEN FALSE

WITH RECURSIVE item_costs AS (
    -- Base case: Direct resource costs and immediate item costs
    SELECT 
        item_id,
        item_price,
        uses_item_id,
        uses_item_price,
        resource_id,
        resource_price,
        quantity,
        0 as depth
    FROM store
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: Expand nested item dependencies
    SELECT 
        ic.item_id,
        ic.item_price,
        s.uses_item_id,
        s.uses_item_price,
        s.resource_id,
        s.resource_price,
        ic.quantity * s.quantity as quantity,
        ic.depth + 1
    FROM item_costs ic
    JOIN store s ON ic.uses_item_id = s.item_id
    WHERE ic.uses_item_id IS NOT NULL
      AND ic.depth < 10  -- Prevent infinite recursion
)
SELECT 
    s.item_id,
    s.item_price as retail_price,
    -- Partial craft price: only direct resources and items (no recursion)
    SUM(DISTINCT CASE 
        WHEN s.resource_id IS NOT NULL THEN s.resource_price * s.quantity 
        ELSE 0 
    END) + 
    SUM(DISTINCT CASE 
        WHEN s.uses_item_id IS NOT NULL THEN s.uses_item_price * s.quantity 
        ELSE 0 
    END) as partial_craft_price,
    -- Full craft price: recursively calculated costs
    COALESCE(
        (SELECT SUM(resource_price * quantity) 
         FROM item_costs 
         WHERE item_costs.item_id = s.item_id 
           AND resource_id IS NOT NULL), 0
    ) as full_craft_price
FROM store s
GROUP BY s.item_id, s.item_price
ORDER BY s.item_id

WITH RECURSIVE EmpMgrCTE AS (
    -- Base case: Start with all employees and their direct managers
    SELECT 
        e.id as employeeId,
        e.salary as employeeSalary,
        1 as hierarchical_level,
        e.bossid as bossId,
        m.salary as bossSalary,
        CASE WHEN m.salary >= 2 * e.salary THEN 1 ELSE 0 END as found_qualifying_boss
    FROM staff e
    LEFT JOIN staff m ON e.bossid = m.id
    WHERE e.bossid IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: Move up the hierarchy until we find a qualifying boss
    SELECT 
        cte.employeeId,
        cte.employeeSalary,
        cte.hierarchical_level + 1,
        m.bossid as bossId,
        mb.salary as bossSalary,
        CASE WHEN mb.salary >= 2 * cte.employeeSalary THEN 1 ELSE 0 END as found_qualifying_boss
    FROM EmpMgrCTE cte
    INNER JOIN staff m ON cte.bossId = m.id
    LEFT JOIN staff mb ON m.bossid = mb.id
    WHERE cte.found_qualifying_boss = 0  -- Only continue if we haven't found a qualifying boss yet
      AND m.bossid IS NOT NULL  -- Stop if there's no higher manager
)
SELECT DISTINCT ON (employeeId)
    employeeId,
    employeeSalary,
    hierarchical_level,
    bossId,
    bossSalary
FROM EmpMgrCTE
WHERE found_qualifying_boss = 1  -- Only return rows where we found a qualifying boss
ORDER BY employeeId, hierarchical_level  -- Get the lowest level (first occurrence) for each employee

WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (
  -- Base case: first row
  SELECT 
    id, 
    amount, 
    amount as cum_sum, 
    1 as bucket 
  FROM transfer 
  WHERE id = 1
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive case: subsequent rows
  SELECT 
    transfer.id, 
    transfer.amount,
    CASE 
      WHEN cte.cum_sum + transfer.amount > 20 THEN transfer.amount 
      ELSE cte.cum_sum + transfer.amount 
    END as cum_sum,
    CASE 
      WHEN cte.cum_sum + transfer.amount > 20 THEN cte.bucket + 1 
      ELSE cte.bucket 
    END as bucket
  FROM cte 
  JOIN transfer ON transfer.id = cte.id + 1
)
SELECT 
  id, 
  amount,
  cum_sum, 
  bucket 
FROM cte
ORDER BY id

WITH lap_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        raceid,
        laps,
        COUNT(*) as frequency,
        MIN(resultid) as first_occurrence
    FROM results
    GROUP BY raceid, laps
),
max_frequency AS (
    SELECT 
        raceid,
        MAX(frequency) as max_freq
    FROM lap_counts
    GROUP BY raceid
)
SELECT 
    lc.raceid,
    lc.laps as modal_laps
FROM lap_counts lc
JOIN max_frequency mf ON lc.raceid = mf.raceid AND lc.frequency = mf.max_freq
WHERE lc.first_occurrence = (
    SELECT MIN(first_occurrence)
    FROM lap_counts lc2
    JOIN max_frequency mf2 ON lc2.raceid = mf2.raceid AND lc2.frequency = mf2.max_freq
    WHERE lc2.raceid = lc.raceid
)

   OR t2.table_name IS NULL;  -- Missing tables from schema

WITH hero_attribute(hero_id, attribute_id, attribute_value) AS (
    VALUES (1, 1, 80), (2, 1, 75), (3, 1, 95), (4, 1, 85), (5, 1, 90), (6, 1, 70)
),
base_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_value,
        ((hero_id - 1) / 2) AS base_group
    FROM hero_attribute
),
expanded_groups AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        bg1.base_group AS group_id,
        bg2.hero_id,
        bg2.attribute_value
    FROM base_groups bg1
    CROSS JOIN base_groups bg2
    WHERE bg2.hero_id BETWEEN (bg1.base_group * 2 + 1 - 2) AND (bg1.base_group * 2 + 2 + 2)
)
SELECT SUM(attribute_value) AS sum
FROM expanded_groups
GROUP BY group_id
ORDER BY group_id

        1 AS source,

), 
birth_year_summary AS (
    SELECT 
        2 AS source, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(*), LEFT(birthday, 4)) AS row_number, 
        CONCAT('There are a total of ', COUNT(*), ' player', CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 1 THEN 's' ELSE '' END, ' born in ', LEFT(birthday, 4), '.') AS dest 
    FROM player_table 
    GROUP BY LEFT(birthday, 4)

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to() 
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$ 
BEGIN 
    -- Update eff_to for the newly inserted team's records
    UPDATE team_attributes ta
    SET eff_to = (
        SELECT COALESCE(
            LEAD(TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE, 1) 
            OVER (
                PARTITION BY team_api_id 
                ORDER BY TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE
            ),
            TO_DATE('5999-12-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ) - 1
        FROM team_attributes ta2
        WHERE ta2.id = ta.id
          AND ta2.team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
    )
    WHERE ta.team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id;
    
    RETURN NEW;
END; 
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER after_insert_team_attributes 
AFTER INSERT ON team_attributes 
FOR EACH ROW 
EXECUTE PROCEDURE update_team_attributes_eff_to()

    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT raceid) > 1
)

WITH RECURSIVE cte AS (
    -- Base case: Start with leaf nodes (nodes that have color already set)
    SELECT id, parent_id, color
    FROM superhero2
    WHERE color IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: Process parent nodes based on their children's colors
    SELECT 
        s.id, 
        s.parent_id,
        CASE 
            -- If parent has only one child, take that child's color
            WHEN COUNT(c.id) = 1 THEN MAX(c.color)
            -- If parent has two children with same color, take that color
            WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT c.color) = 1 THEN MAX(c.color)
            -- If parent has two children with different colors, make it gray
            ELSE 'gray'
        END AS color
    FROM superhero2 s
    INNER JOIN cte c ON s.id = c.parent_id
    WHERE s.color IS NULL
    GROUP BY s.id, s.parent_id
)
UPDATE superhero2
SET color = cte.color
FROM cte
WHERE superhero2.id = cte.id AND superhero2.color IS NULL

    at.total_cards
FROM cards c
INNER JOIN artist_totals at ON c.artist = at.artist
GROUP BY c.artist, c.setcode, at.total_cards
ORDER BY at.total_cards DESC, c.artist, counter DESC

SELECT 
    match_id, 
    current_lineup, 
    next_lineup,
    COALESCE(array_length(ARRAY(
        SELECT DISTINCT unnest(current_lineup)
        INTERSECT
        SELECT DISTINCT unnest(next_lineup)
    ), 1), 0) AS players_in_both_lineups
FROM match_lineups

WITH activity_changes AS (
  SELECT 
    ts,
    superhero_id,
    activity_code,
    LAG(ts) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) as prev_ts,
    CASE 
      WHEN LAG(ts) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) IS NULL 
           OR ts - LAG(ts) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) > INTERVAL '5 minutes'
      THEN 1 
      ELSE 0 
    END as is_new_group
  FROM superhero_activities
),
activity_groups AS (
  SELECT 
    ts,
    superhero_id,
    activity_code,
    SUM(is_new_group) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) as group_id
  FROM activity_changes
)
SELECT 
  superhero_id,
  activity_code,
  MIN(ts) as start_ts,
  MAX(ts) as end_ts,
  (EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (MAX(ts) - MIN(ts))) / 60 + 5) as total_minutes
FROM activity_groups
GROUP BY superhero_id, activity_code, group_id
ORDER BY superhero_id, activity_code, start_ts

WITH RECURSIVE card_hierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: root nodes
    SELECT
        uuid,
        card_name,
        parent_uuid,
        0 AS level
    FROM card_type
    WHERE parent_uuid IS NULL

    UNION ALL

    -- Recursive case: get all descendants
    SELECT
        c.uuid,
        c.card_name,
        c.parent_uuid,
        ch.level + 1
    FROM card_type c
    JOIN card_hierarchy ch ON c.parent_uuid = ch.uuid
)
SELECT
    parent.uuid,
    parent.card_name AS name,
    parent.parent_uuid,
    COALESCE(
        array_agg(
            child.uuid
            ORDER BY child.card_name
        ) FILTER (WHERE child.uuid IS NOT NULL),
        ARRAY[]::uuid[]
    ) AS children_uuids,
    COALESCE(
        json_agg(
            json_build_object(
                'uuid', child.uuid,
                'card_name', child.card_name
            )
            ORDER BY child.card_name
        ) FILTER (WHERE child.uuid IS NOT NULL),
        '[]'::json
    ) AS children_details
FROM card_type parent
LEFT JOIN card_type child ON child.parent_uuid = parent.uuid
GROUP BY parent.uuid, parent.card_name, parent.parent_uuid
ORDER BY parent.card_name

WITH date_series AS (
    SELECT DATE('2020-01-01', '+' || (value - 1) || ' days') AS the_day
    FROM (
        WITH RECURSIVE cnt(value) AS (
            SELECT 1
            UNION ALL
            SELECT value + 1 FROM cnt WHERE value < 91
        )
        SELECT value FROM cnt
    )
)
SELECT 
    d.the_day AS "Date",
    COUNT(e.event_id) AS "Number of Events",
    AVG(b.remaining) AS "Avg Remaining Budget"
FROM date_series d
LEFT JOIN event e 
    ON DATE(e.event_date) = d.the_day 
    AND e.status IN ('Open', 'Closed')
LEFT JOIN budget b 
    ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id
GROUP BY d.the_day
ORDER BY d.the_day

    SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM trans.date) = 1995 THEN trans.amount ELSE 0 END) AS a1995,
    SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM trans.date) = 1996 THEN trans.amount ELSE 0 END) AS a1996,
    SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM trans.date) = 1997 THEN trans.amount ELSE 0 END) AS a1997,
    SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM trans.date) = 1998 THEN trans.amount ELSE 0 END) AS a1998
FROM trans
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month

WITH RECURSIVE calculated_results AS (
    -- Base case: first row (ordered by id)
    SELECT 
        id,
        trx,
        event,
        result_good,
        ROUND((trx * event)::numeric, 6) AS result,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY id) AS rn
    FROM sales_transactions
    WHERE id = (SELECT MIN(id) FROM sales_transactions)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: each subsequent row
    SELECT 
        st.id,
        st.trx,
        st.event,
        st.result_good,
        ROUND(((cr.result + st.trx) * st.event)::numeric, 6) AS result,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY st.id) AS rn
    FROM sales_transactions st
    INNER JOIN calculated_results cr ON st.id > cr.id
    WHERE st.id = (
        SELECT MIN(id) 
        FROM sales_transactions 
        WHERE id > cr.id
    )
)
SELECT 
    id,
    result
FROM calculated_results
ORDER BY id

WITH sorted_circuits AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    -- Sort and concatenate circuit names for group A
    TRIM(
      CASE 
        WHEN circui_name_a1 <= COALESCE(circui_name_a2, '~') AND circui_name_a1 <= COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '~') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_a1, '')
        WHEN circui_name_a2 <= COALESCE(circui_name_a1, '~') AND circui_name_a2 <= COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '~') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_a2, '')
        ELSE COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '')
      END || ' ' ||
      CASE 
        WHEN (circui_name_a1 > COALESCE(circui_name_a2, '~') OR circui_name_a1 > COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '~')) 
          AND (circui_name_a1 < COALESCE(circui_name_a2, '~') OR circui_name_a1 < COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '~'))
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_a1, '')
        WHEN (circui_name_a2 > COALESCE(circui_name_a1, '~') OR circui_name_a2 > COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '~')) 
          AND (circui_name_a2 < COALESCE(circui_name_a1, '~') OR circui_name_a2 < COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '~'))
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_a2, '')
        ELSE COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '')
      END || ' ' ||
      CASE 
        WHEN circui_name_a1 >= COALESCE(circui_name_a2, '') AND circui_name_a1 >= COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_a1, '')
        WHEN circui_name_a2 >= COALESCE(circui_name_a1, '') AND circui_name_a2 >= COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_a2, '')
        ELSE COALESCE(circui_name_a3, '')
      END
    ) AS a,
    -- Sort and concatenate circuit names for group B
    TRIM(
      CASE 
        WHEN circui_name_b1 <= COALESCE(circui_name_b2, '~') AND circui_name_b1 <= COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '~') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_b1, '')
        WHEN circui_name_b2 <= COALESCE(circui_name_b1, '~') AND circui_name_b2 <= COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '~') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_b2, '')
        ELSE COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '')
      END || ' ' ||
      CASE 
        WHEN (circui_name_b1 > COALESCE(circui_name_b2, '~') OR circui_name_b1 > COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '~')) 
          AND (circui_name_b1 < COALESCE(circui_name_b2, '~') OR circui_name_b1 < COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '~'))
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_b1, '')
        WHEN (circui_name_b2 > COALESCE(circui_name_b1, '~') OR circui_name_b2 > COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '~')) 
          AND (circui_name_b2 < COALESCE(circui_name_b1, '~') OR circui_name_b2 < COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '~'))
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_b2, '')
        ELSE COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '')
      END || ' ' ||
      CASE 
        WHEN circui_name_b1 >= COALESCE(circui_name_b2, '') AND circui_name_b1 >= COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_b1, '')
        WHEN circui_name_b2 >= COALESCE(circui_name_b1, '') AND circui_name_b2 >= COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '') 
          THEN COALESCE(circui_name_b2, '')
        ELSE COALESCE(circui_name_b3, '')
      END
    ) AS b
  FROM race_info
)
SELECT 
  a,
  b,
  CASE WHEN a = b THEN 'MATCH' ELSE 'DIFFERENT' END AS comparison
FROM sorted_circuits

WITH daily_prices AS (
    SELECT 
        card_id,
        DATE(date_value) as trading_day,
        date_value,
        price,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY card_id, DATE(date_value) ORDER BY date_value ASC) as rn_first,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY card_id, DATE(date_value) ORDER BY date_value DESC) as rn_last_within_15min
    FROM card_prices
    WHERE EXTRACT(HOUR FROM date_value) * 60 + EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM date_value) <= 
          (EXTRACT(HOUR FROM (SELECT MIN(date_value) FROM card_prices cp2 WHERE cp2.card_id = card_prices.card_id AND DATE(cp2.date_value) = DATE(card_prices.date_value))) * 60 + 
           EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM (SELECT MIN(date_value) FROM card_prices cp2 WHERE cp2.card_id = card_prices.card_id AND DATE(cp2.date_value) = DATE(card_prices.date_value)))) + 15
),
first_prices AS (
    SELECT card_id, trading_day, price as first_price
    FROM daily_prices
    WHERE rn_first = 1
),
last_prices_15min AS (
    SELECT 
        card_id, 
        trading_day, 
        price as last_price_15min
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            card_id,
            DATE(date_value) as trading_day,
            date_value,
            price,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY card_id, DATE(date_value) ORDER BY date_value DESC) as rn
        FROM card_prices cp
        WHERE date_value <= (
            SELECT MIN(date_value) + INTERVAL '15 minutes'
            FROM card_prices cp2
            WHERE cp2.card_id = cp.card_id 
            AND DATE(cp2.date_value) = DATE(cp.date_value)
        )
    ) sub
    WHERE rn = 1
),
daily_differences AS (
    SELECT 
        fp.card_id,
        fp.trading_day,
        lp.last_price_15min - fp.first_price as price_difference
    FROM first_prices fp
    INNER JOIN last_prices_15min lp 
        ON fp.card_id = lp.card_id 
        AND fp.trading_day = lp.trading_day
)
SELECT 
    card_id,
    AVG(price_difference) as avg_difference
FROM daily_differences
GROUP BY card_id
ORDER BY card_id

SELECT

INNER JOIN connected c1 ON b1.bond_id = c1.bond_id
INNER JOIN connected c2 ON (c1.atom_id2 = c2.atom_id OR c1.atom_id = c2.atom_id)
INNER JOIN bond b2 ON c2.bond_id = b2.bond_id

    AND b1.molecule_id = b2.molecule_id

ORDER BY b1.bond_id, b2.bond_id

create or replace function setup_vector_store(table_name text) 
returns void 
language plpgsql 
as $outer$ 
begin 
    -- Drop the existing function if it exists
    execute 'drop function if exists match_strategies(int, jsonb)';
    
    -- Create the table with embedding column
    execute format(
        'create table %I ( 
            id bigint primary key generated always as identity, 
            description text, 
            metadata jsonb,
            embedding vector
        )', 
        table_name
    );
    
    -- Create the search function with proper nested dollar quoting
    execute format(
        'create function match_strategies ( 
            query_embedding vector,
            match_count int default null, 
            filter jsonb default ''{}''::jsonb 
        ) 
        returns table ( 
            id bigint, 
            description text, 
            metadata jsonb, 
            similarity float 
        ) 
        language plpgsql 
        as $inner$ 
        begin 
            return query 
            select 
                t.id, 
                t.description, 
                t.metadata, 
                1 - (t.embedding <=> query_embedding) as similarity 
            from %I t
            where t.metadata @> filter 
            order by t.embedding <=> query_embedding 
            limit match_count; 
        end; 
        $inner$;', 
        table_name
    );
end; 
$outer$

WITH datapoint_variables AS (
    SELECT 
        dv.datapoint_id,
        MAX(CASE WHEN v.name = 'ProductType' THEN vv.name END) AS product_type,
        MAX(CASE WHEN v.name = 'publishing_language' THEN vv.name END) AS language,
        MAX(CASE WHEN v.name = 'genre' THEN vv.name END) AS genre
    FROM datapoint_variablevalue dv
    JOIN variablevalue vv ON dv.variablevalue_id = vv.id
    JOIN variable v ON vv.variable_id = v.id
    GROUP BY dv.datapoint_id
),
all_combinations AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        td.years,
        sd.name AS country,
        vv1.name AS product_type,
        vv2.name AS language,
        vv3.name AS genre
    FROM time_dimension td
    CROSS JOIN spatial_dimension sd
    CROSS JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT name FROM variablevalue WHERE variable_id = 1) vv1
    CROSS JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT name FROM variablevalue WHERE variable_id = 2) vv2
    CROSS JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT name FROM variablevalue WHERE variable_id = 3) vv3
)
SELECT 
    ac.years,
    ac.country,
    ac.product_type,
    ac.language,
    ac.genre,
    COALESCE(SUM(dp.value), 0) AS total_sales
FROM all_combinations ac
LEFT JOIN time_dimension td ON ac.years = td.years
LEFT JOIN spatial_dimension sd ON ac.country = sd.name
LEFT JOIN datapoints dp ON dp.time_dimension_id = td.id AND dp.spatial_dimension_id = sd.id
LEFT JOIN datapoint_variables dv ON dp.id = dv.datapoint_id 
    AND ac.product_type = dv.product_type 
    AND ac.language = dv.language 
    AND ac.genre = dv.genre
GROUP BY ac.years, ac.country, ac.product_type, ac.language, ac.genre
ORDER BY ac.years DESC, ac.country, ac.product_type, ac.language, ac.genre

SELECT 
    schemaname || '.' || tablename AS table_name,
    pg_size_pretty(pg_total_relation_size(schemaname || '.' || tablename)) AS table_size,
    pg_total_relation_size(schemaname || '.' || tablename) AS size_bytes
FROM pg_tables
WHERE schemaname = 'public'
ORDER BY pg_total_relation_size(schemaname || '.' || tablename) DESC

WITH split_events AS (
  SELECT string_to_array(event_description, '#') AS tokens
  FROM match_events
),
min_length AS (
  SELECT MIN(array_length(tokens, 1)) AS min_len
  FROM split_events
),
position_check AS (
  SELECT 
    pos,
    COUNT(DISTINCT tokens[pos]) = 1 AS is_common
  FROM split_events
  CROSS JOIN generate_series(1, (SELECT min_len FROM min_length)) AS pos
  GROUP BY pos
)
SELECT 
  array_to_string(
    (SELECT tokens[1:COALESCE((
      SELECT MAX(pos) 
      FROM position_check 
      WHERE is_common 
        AND NOT EXISTS (
          SELECT 1 FROM position_check pc2 
          WHERE pc2.pos < position_check.pos AND NOT pc2.is_common
        )
    ), 0)]
    FROM split_events LIMIT 1),
    '#'
  ) AS common_prefix

WITH molecule_totals AS (
    SELECT 
        molecule_id,
        SUM((bond_stat_->>'time')::numeric) AS total_time
    FROM bond
    WHERE bond_stat_->>'time' IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY molecule_id
)
SELECT 
    b.bond_id,
    b.molecule_id,
    b.bond_stat_->>'type' AS bond_type,
    (b.bond_stat_->>'time')::numeric AS bond_time,
    mt.total_time,
    (mt.total_time * 0.1) AS threshold
FROM bond b
INNER JOIN molecule_totals mt ON b.molecule_id = mt.molecule_id
WHERE 
    b.bond_stat_->>'type' = 'Unknown'
    AND (b.bond_stat_->>'time')::numeric > (mt.total_time * 0.1)

WITH monthly_periods AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    rate,
    generate_series(
      DATE_TRUNC('day', LOWER(time_span)),
      COALESCE(DATE_TRUNC('day', UPPER(time_span)), CURRENT_DATE),
      '1 month'::INTERVAL
    ) AS period_start
  FROM budgets
),
period_details AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    rate,
    period_start::DATE AS period_start,
    (LEAD(period_start, 1, COALESCE(
      (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('day', UPPER(time_span)) FROM budgets WHERE budgets.id = monthly_periods.id),
      CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '1 day'
    )) OVER (PARTITION BY id ORDER BY period_start))::DATE AS period_end
  FROM monthly_periods
),
daily_series AS (
  SELECT
    id,
    rate,
    period_start,
    period_end,
    (period_end - period_start) AS days_in_period,
    generate_series(period_start, period_end - INTERVAL '1 day', '1 day'::INTERVAL)::DATE AS date
  FROM period_details
)
SELECT 
  date,
  rate / days_in_period AS daily_rate
FROM daily_series
ORDER BY date

WITH RECURSIVE time_series AS (
  -- Generate hourly intervals for each event
  SELECT 
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    start_timestamp,
    end_timestamp,
    DATE_TRUNC('hour', start_timestamp) AS hour_start,
    DATE_TRUNC('hour', start_timestamp) + INTERVAL '1 hour' AS hour_end
  FROM event_usage
  
  UNION ALL
  
  SELECT 
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    start_timestamp,
    end_timestamp,
    hour_end AS hour_start,
    hour_end + INTERVAL '1 hour' AS hour_end
  FROM time_series
  WHERE hour_end < end_timestamp
),
working_hours_calc AS (
  SELECT 
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    start_timestamp,
    end_timestamp,
    hour_start,
    hour_end,
    -- Check if this hour falls within working hours (9:00-18:00) and weekdays (1=Monday, 5=Friday)
    EXTRACT(DOW FROM hour_start) AS day_of_week,
    EXTRACT(HOUR FROM hour_start) AS hour_of_day,
    -- Calculate the actual overlap with the event
    GREATEST(hour_start, start_timestamp) AS actual_start,
    LEAST(hour_end, end_timestamp) AS actual_end
  FROM time_series
  WHERE 
    EXTRACT(DOW FROM hour_start) BETWEEN 1 AND 5  -- Monday to Friday
    AND EXTRACT(HOUR FROM hour_start) BETWEEN 9 AND 17  -- 9:00-17:59 (covers 9:00-18:00)
)
SELECT 
  learner_code,
  resource_key,
  SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (actual_end - actual_start))) AS total_working_hours_seconds
FROM working_hours_calc
GROUP BY learner_code, resource_key
ORDER BY learner_code, resource_key

WITH numbered_ratings AS (
  SELECT 
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    usage_date,
    rating,
    -- Create groups where each non-null rating starts a new group
    SUM(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (
      PARTITION BY learner_code, resource_key 
      ORDER BY usage_date 
      ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) AS rating_group
  FROM resource_usage_2
)
SELECT 
  learner_code,
  resource_key,
  usage_date,
  rating,
  FIRST_VALUE(rating) OVER (
    PARTITION BY learner_code, resource_key, rating_group 
    ORDER BY usage_date 
    ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
  ) AS filled_rating
FROM numbered_ratings
ORDER BY learner_code, resource_key, usage_date

WITH date_series AS (
    SELECT dd::date AS day_start
    FROM generate_series('2022-01-01'::timestamp, '2022-01-31'::timestamp, '1 day'::interval) dd
),
converted_incidents AS (
    SELECT 
        to_timestamp(incident_start / 1000) AS incident_start,
        to_timestamp(incident_end / 1000) AS incident_end
    FROM race_incidents
    WHERE incident_start IS NOT NULL AND incident_end IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    ds.day_start,
    COALESCE(SUM(
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (
            LEAST(ci.incident_end, (ds.day_start + INTERVAL '1 day')::timestamp) -
            GREATEST(ci.incident_start, ds.day_start::timestamp)
        )) / 60
    ), 0) AS incident_minutes
FROM date_series ds
LEFT JOIN converted_incidents ci 
    ON ci.incident_start < (ds.day_start + INTERVAL '1 day')::timestamp
    AND ci.incident_end > ds.day_start::timestamp
GROUP BY ds.day_start
ORDER BY ds.day_start

WITH ranked_labs AS (

    FROM laboratory l
)
SELECT 
    p.id,
    p.sex,
    p.birthday,
    p.diagnosis,
    MAX(CASE WHEN rn = 1 THEN ldh END) as ldh_1,
    MAX(CASE WHEN rn = 1 THEN alp END) as alp_1,
    MAX(CASE WHEN rn = 2 THEN ldh END) as ldh_2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN rn = 2 THEN alp END) as alp_2,
    MAX(CASE WHEN rn = 3 THEN ldh END) as ldh_3,
    MAX(CASE WHEN rn = 3 THEN alp END) as alp_3
FROM patient p
LEFT JOIN ranked_labs rl ON p.id = rl.id AND rl.rn <= 3
GROUP BY p.id, p.sex, p.birthday, p.diagnosis
ORDER BY p.id

        CASE

        END AS priority
    FROM budget
    WHERE category IN ('Food', 'Advertisement')
        AND amount IS NOT NULL
),
cumulative_budget AS (
    SELECT 
        budget_id,
        category,
        amount,
        SUM(amount) OVER (ORDER BY priority, amount) AS cum_amount
    FROM prioritized_budget
)
SELECT 
    budget_id,
    category,
    amount,
    cum_amount,
    CASE 
        WHEN cum_amount <= 500 THEN 'Can Purchase'
        ELSE 'Exceeds Budget'

ORDER BY cum_amount

WITH update_result AS (
    UPDATE loan
    SET status = 'updated_status',
        date = 'new_date',
        amount = 'new_amount'
    WHERE loan_id = 'loan_id_value'
    AND status = 'current_status'
    RETURNING loan_id, status
),
loan_check AS (
    SELECT 
        loan_id,
        status as current_status
    FROM loan
    WHERE loan_id = 'loan_id_value'
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM update_result) THEN 'successful_update'
        WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM loan_check WHERE current_status != 'current_status') THEN 'version_conflict'
        WHEN NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM loan_check) THEN 'loan_not_found'
        ELSE 'unknown'
    END as outcome,
    COALESCE((SELECT loan_id FROM update_result), (SELECT loan_id FROM loan_check)) as loan_id

WITH RECURSIVE
-- Get buy transactions with running totals
buys AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_id,
        molecule_id,
        date,
        molecules,
        usd_total,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) AS cumulative_bought,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) - molecules AS prev_cumulative_bought
    FROM transaction
    WHERE sell = 0
),
-- Get sell transactions with running totals
sells AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_id,
        molecule_id,
        date,
        molecules,
        usd_total,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) AS cumulative_sold,
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date, transaction_id) - molecules AS prev_cumulative_sold
    FROM transaction
    WHERE sell = 1
),
-- Match buys and sells
matched AS (
    SELECT 
        b.molecule_id,
        b.date AS buy_date,
        s.date AS sell_date,
        b.transaction_id AS buy_transaction_id,
        s.transaction_id AS sell_transaction_id,
        -- Calculate overlap between this buy and this sell
        CASE 
            WHEN b.cumulative_bought <= s.prev_cumulative_sold THEN 0
            WHEN b.prev_cumulative_bought >= s.cumulative_sold THEN 0
            ELSE LEAST(b.cumulative_bought, s.cumulative_sold) - GREATEST(b.prev_cumulative_bought, s.prev_cumulative_sold)
        END AS molecules,
        b.usd_total / b.molecules AS buy_price_per_molecule,
        s.usd_total / s.molecules AS sell_price_per_molecule
    FROM buys b
    JOIN sells s ON b.molecule_id = s.molecule_id
    WHERE b.cumulative_bought > s.prev_cumulative_sold 
      AND b.prev_cumulative_bought < s.cumulative_sold
)
SELECT 
    molecule_id,
    buy_date,
    sell_date,
    molecules,
    ROUND(molecules * buy_price_per_molecule, 2) AS buy_usd_total,
    ROUND(molecules * sell_price_per_molecule, 2) AS sell_usd_total
FROM matched
WHERE molecules > 0
ORDER BY molecule_id, sell_date, buy_date

WITH RECURSIVE weekdays AS (
    SELECT generate_series(0, 60) AS offset
),
dates AS (
    SELECT 
        CURRENT_DATE + (w.offset || ' days')::interval AS date,
        EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM CURRENT_DATE + (w.offset || ' days')::interval) AS day_of_week
    FROM weekdays w
    WHERE EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM CURRENT_DATE + (w.offset || ' days')::interval) IN (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7)
),
schedule_blocks_with_dates AS (
    SELECT 
        sb.*,
        d.date AS block_date
    FROM schedule_blocks sb
    JOIN dates d ON TRIM(sb.day_of_week) ILIKE TRIM(to_char(d.date, 'Day'))
    WHERE sb.is_available = TRUE
        AND d.date >= CURRENT_DATE
        AND d.date <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '2 months'
)
SELECT 
    block_id,
    user_id,
    block_date AS date,
    start_time,
    end_time
FROM schedule_blocks_with_dates
ORDER BY date, start_time

WITH RECURSIVE A(GroupNum, n, RollingSum, id) AS (
    SELECT 
        1 as GroupNum, 
        Performance_Prediction as n, 
        Performance_Prediction as RollingSum, 
        prediction_code as id
    FROM performance_predictions
    WHERE prediction_code = (SELECT MIN(prediction_code) FROM performance_predictions)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN A.RollingSum + P.Performance_Prediction > 200 
            THEN A.GroupNum + 1 
            ELSE A.GroupNum 
        END as GroupNum,
        P.Performance_Prediction as n,
        CASE 
            WHEN A.RollingSum + P.Performance_Prediction > 200
            THEN P.Performance_Prediction 
            ELSE A.RollingSum + P.Performance_Prediction 
        END as RollingSum,
        P.prediction_code as id
    FROM A
    JOIN performance_predictions AS P ON P.prediction_code = (
        SELECT MIN(prediction_code) 
        FROM performance_predictions 
        WHERE prediction_code > A.id
    )
)
SELECT 
    GroupNum, 
    MAX(RollingSum) AS group_sum
FROM A
GROUP BY GroupNum
ORDER BY GroupNum

    followup_no = EXCLUDED.followup_no,
    primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score = EXCLUDED.primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score,
    primary_pathology_eastern_cancer_oncology_group = EXCLUDED.primary_pathology_eastern_cancer_oncology_group,
    primary_pathology_radiation_therapy = EXCLUDED.primary_pathology_radiation_therapy,
    primary_pathology_postoperative_rx_tx = EXCLUDED.primary_pathology_postoperative_rx_tx,
    has_follow_ups_information = EXCLUDED.has_follow_ups_information,
    has_drugs_information = EXCLUDED.has_drugs_information,
    has_radiations_information = EXCLUDED.has_radiations_information,
    project = EXCLUDED.project,
    day_of_form_completion = EXCLUDED.day_of_form_completion,
    month_of_form_completion = EXCLUDED.month_of_form_completion,
    year_of_form_completion = EXCLUDED.year_of_form_completion;

    AVG(o.Pre_Test_Score) AS average_pre_test_score
FROM learners l
LEFT JOIN outcomes o ON l.learner_code = o.learner_code
WHERE 
    l.Engagement_History > 5 
    AND l.Gender = 'Female' 
    AND l.Language_Proficiency IN ('Basic', 'Intermediate')
    AND (
        (l.Socioeconomic_Background IN ('Low', 'Middle') AND l.Geographic_Location = 'Urban')
        OR 
        (l.Socioeconomic_Background = 'High' AND l.Geographic_Location = 'Rural')
    )
GROUP BY l.learner_code, l.Geographic_Location

WITH RECURSIVE date_range AS (
    SELECT 
        TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36' AS start_ts,
        TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24' AS end_ts,
        DATE(TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36') AS current_date,
        DATE(TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24') AS end_date
),
weekday_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        start_ts,
        end_ts,
        -- Calculate full weekdays between dates
        (SELECT COUNT(*) 
         FROM generate_series(
             DATE(start_ts) + INTERVAL '1 day',
             DATE(end_ts) - INTERVAL '1 day',
             INTERVAL '1 day'
         ) AS d
         WHERE EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM d) < 6
        ) AS full_weekdays,
        -- Handle start date
        CASE 
            WHEN EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM start_ts) < 6 THEN 
                EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (DATE(start_ts) + INTERVAL '1 day' - start_ts))
            ELSE 0
        END AS start_day_seconds,
        -- Handle end date
        CASE 
            WHEN EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM end_ts) < 6 THEN 
                EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (end_ts - DATE(end_ts)))
            ELSE 0
        END AS end_day_seconds
    FROM date_range
),
total_seconds AS (
    SELECT 
        (full_weekdays * 86400 + start_day_seconds + end_day_seconds) AS total_secs
    FROM weekday_calc
)
SELECT 
    FLOOR(total_secs / 86400) || ' days ' ||
    FLOOR((total_secs % 86400) / 3600) || ' hours ' ||
    FLOOR((total_secs % 3600) / 60) || ' minutes ' ||
    FLOOR(total_secs % 60) || ' seconds' AS time_diff
FROM total_seconds

WITH links AS (
    SELECT 
        greatest(link.source_cluster, link.target_cluster) AS big, 
        least(link.source_cluster, link.target_cluster) AS smol, 
        link.type AS type 
    FROM link
) 
SELECT 
    l.cluster AS left_id, 
    l.cluster_type AS left_type, 
    l.cluster_label AS left_label, 
    r.cluster AS right_id, 
    r.cluster_type AS right_type, 
    r.cluster_label AS right_label, 
    count(DISTINCT l.article) AS articles, 
    array_agg(DISTINCT links.type) FILTER (WHERE links.type IS NOT NULL) AS link_types
FROM tag AS l
JOIN tag AS r 
    ON l.article = r.article 
    AND l.cluster > r.cluster
LEFT JOIN links 
    ON l.cluster = links.big 
    AND r.cluster = links.smol
GROUP BY 
    l.cluster, 
    l.cluster_label, 
    l.cluster_type, 
    r.cluster, 
    r.cluster_label, 
    r.cluster_type
ORDER BY count(DISTINCT l.article) DESC

-- First, update the records to keep with consolidated date ranges
UPDATE sp_album_ctb
SET start_date = c.new_start_date,
    end_date = c.new_end_date
FROM (
  -- Same CTE logic as above to find consolidated records
  WITH ordered_records AS (
    SELECT id, alb, art, feat_arts, start_date, end_date,
           LAG(end_date) OVER (PARTITION BY alb, art, feat_arts ORDER BY start_date) AS prev_end_date
    FROM sp_album_ctb
  ),
  groups AS (
    SELECT id, alb, art, feat_arts, start_date, end_date,
           CASE WHEN prev_end_date IS NULL THEN 1
                WHEN start_date = prev_end_date + INTERVAL '1 day' THEN 0
                ELSE 1 END AS is_new_group
    FROM ordered_records
  ),
  group_numbers AS (
    SELECT id, alb, art, feat_arts, start_date, end_date,
           SUM(is_new_group) OVER (PARTITION BY alb, art, feat_arts ORDER BY start_date) AS group_id
    FROM groups
  )
  SELECT MIN(id) AS keep_id, MIN(start_date) AS new_start_date, MAX(end_date) AS new_end_date
  FROM group_numbers
  GROUP BY alb, art, feat_arts, group_id
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
) c
WHERE sp_album_ctb.id = c.keep_id;

-- Then delete the redundant records
DELETE FROM sp_album_ctb
WHERE id IN (
  WITH ordered_records AS (
    SELECT id, alb, art, feat_arts, start_date, end_date,
           LAG(end_date) OVER (PARTITION BY alb, art, feat_arts ORDER BY start_date) AS prev_end_date
    FROM sp_album_ctb
  ),
  groups AS (
    SELECT id, alb, art, feat_arts, start_date, end_date,
           CASE WHEN prev_end_date IS NULL THEN 1
                WHEN start_date = prev_end_date + INTERVAL '1 day' THEN 0
                ELSE 1 END AS is_new_group
    FROM ordered_records
  ),
  group_numbers AS (
    SELECT id, alb, art, feat_arts, start_date, end_date,
           SUM(is_new_group) OVER (PARTITION BY alb, art, feat_arts ORDER BY start_date) AS group_id
    FROM groups
  )
  SELECT UNNEST(ARRAY_AGG(id ORDER BY start_date)[2:]) AS id_to_delete
  FROM group_numbers
  GROUP BY alb, art, feat_arts, group_id
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)

WITH sales AS (

), 
initial_purchase AS (
    SELECT 
        "GasstationId", 
        "CustomerId", 
        MIN(CAST("SaleDate" AS date)) AS "FirstPurchaseDate" 
    FROM sales 
    GROUP BY "GasstationId", "CustomerId"
), 
customer_wages AS (
    SELECT 
        i."CustomerId", 
        s.* 
    FROM sales s 
    JOIN initial_purchase i 
        ON s."CustomerId" = i."CustomerId" 
        AND s."GasstationId" = i."GasstationId"
    WHERE s."SaleDate" >= i."FirstPurchaseDate" 
    ORDER BY i."CustomerId" DESC
)

WITH RankedPrices AS (
    SELECT 
        product_id,
        price,
        effective_from,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY product_id ORDER BY effective_from DESC) as rn
    FROM ProductPrice
)
SELECT DISTINCT
    p.product_id,
    p.product_name
FROM Product p
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM RankedPrices rp1
    JOIN RankedPrices rp2 ON rp1.product_id = rp2.product_id
    WHERE rp1.product_id = p.product_id
        AND rp1.rn = 1  -- most recent price
        AND rp2.rn = 2  -- previous price
        AND rp1.price < rp2.price  -- most recent is lower than previous
)
ORDER BY p.product_id

SELECT 
    CD.customer_id,
    COALESCE(S.total_sales, 0) AS total_sales,
    COALESCE(S.total_amount, 0) AS total_amount,
    COALESCE(C.total_communications, 0) AS total_communications,
    COALESCE(F.total_feedback, 0) AS total_feedback,
    COALESCE(F.total_positive_feedback, 0) AS total_positive_feedback,
    COALESCE(F.total_negative_feedback, 0) AS total_negative_feedback
FROM customer_details CD
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        COUNT(*) AS total_sales,
        SUM(amount) AS total_amount
    FROM sales
    GROUP BY customer_id
) S ON CD.customer_id = S.customer_id
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        COUNT(*) AS total_communications
    FROM communications
    GROUP BY customer_id
) C ON CD.customer_id = C.customer_id
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        COUNT(*) AS total_feedback,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE feedback_type = 'positive') AS total_positive_feedback,
        COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE feedback_type = 'negative') AS total_negative_feedback
    FROM feedback
    GROUP BY customer_id
) F ON CD.customer_id = F.customer_id

-- Remove the CHECK constraint and use a TRIGGER instead

-- Trigger for INSERT operations
CREATE TRIGGER prevent_self_reference_insert
BEFORE INSERT ON couple
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
  IF NEW.id = NEW.spouseid THEN
    SIGNAL SQLSTATE '45000'
    SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'A record cannot reference itself: id cannot equal spouseid';
  END IF;
END;

-- Trigger for UPDATE operations
CREATE TRIGGER prevent_self_reference_update
BEFORE UPDATE ON couple
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
  IF NEW.id = NEW.spouseid THEN
    SIGNAL SQLSTATE '45000'
    SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'A record cannot reference itself: id cannot equal spouseid';
  END IF;
END

SELECT 
    TRIM(BOTH ', ' FROM 
        CONCAT(
            CASE WHEN HOUR(Description) > 0 THEN CONCAT(HOUR(Description), ' hour', IF(HOUR(Description) > 1, 's', ''), ', ') ELSE '' END,
            CASE WHEN MINUTE(Description) > 0 THEN CONCAT(MINUTE(Description), ' minute', IF(MINUTE(Description) > 1, 's', ''), ', ') ELSE '' END,
            CASE WHEN SECOND(Description) > 0 THEN CONCAT(SECOND(Description), ' second', IF(SECOND(Description) > 1, 's', '')) ELSE '' END
        )
    ) AS formatted_duration
FROM backup_colour
WHERE Description IS NOT NULL

CREATE TABLE `log_content` (
  `log_id` int(11) NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
  `content` varchar(32) NOT NULL,
  `no_of_chars` int(4) NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`log_id`)
);

DELIMITER $$
CREATE TRIGGER `set_no_of_chars_before_insert`
BEFORE INSERT ON `log_content`
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
  SET NEW.no_of_chars = CHAR_LENGTH(NEW.content);
END$$

CREATE TRIGGER `set_no_of_chars_before_update`
BEFORE UPDATE ON `log_content`
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
  SET NEW.no_of_chars = CHAR_LENGTH(NEW.content);
END$$

SELECT 
    cr.release_id, 
    cr.code_id, 
    cr.code_status_id, 
    cr.code_created_date 
FROM card_release cr
INNER JOIN (
    SELECT 
        code_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN code_status_id = 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 THEN 3
            ELSE 2
        END AS priority_status,
        MAX(CASE WHEN code_status_id = 3 THEN code_created_date END) AS paid_date,
        MAX(CASE WHEN code_status_id = 2 THEN code_created_date END) AS requesting_date
    FROM card_release
    GROUP BY code_id
) priority ON cr.code_id = priority.code_id
WHERE 
    (priority.priority_status = 3 AND cr.code_status_id = 3 AND cr.code_created_date = priority.paid_date)
    OR 
    (priority.priority_status = 2 AND cr.code_status_id = 2 AND cr.code_created_date = priority.requesting_date)

WITH RECURSIVE HierarchyCTE AS (
    -- Base case: Start with leaf nodes (nodes that are not parents of any other node)
    SELECT 
        entityid, 
        parentid, 
        emission,
        CAST(emission AS DECIMAL(20,2)) AS cumulative_total
    FROM tree_table
    WHERE entityid NOT IN (
        SELECT DISTINCT parentid 
        FROM tree_table 
        WHERE parentid IS NOT NULL
    )
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: Process parent nodes
    SELECT 
        t.entityid,
        t.parentid,
        t.emission,
        CAST(t.emission + SUM(cte.cumulative_total * cte.pctcontribution / 100.0) AS DECIMAL(20,2)) AS cumulative_total
    FROM tree_table t
    INNER JOIN HierarchyCTE cte ON t.entityid = cte.parentid
    GROUP BY t.entityid, t.parentid, t.emission
)
SELECT 
    entityid,
    parentid,
    cumulative_total
FROM HierarchyCTE
ORDER BY entityid

WITH AllWorkers AS (
    SELECT id FROM Worker1 WHERE id IS NOT NULL
    UNION
    SELECT id FROM Worker2 WHERE id IS NOT NULL
),
MaxID AS (
    SELECT COALESCE(MAX(id), 0) AS max_val FROM AllWorkers
)
SELECT CASE 
    WHEN NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM AllWorkers WHERE id = 1) THEN 1
    ELSE COALESCE(
        (SELECT MIN(a.id + 1)
         FROM AllWorkers a
         WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM AllWorkers b WHERE b.id = a.id + 1)
         AND a.id + 1 <= (SELECT max_val FROM MaxID)),
        (SELECT max_val + 1 FROM MaxID)
    )
END AS lowest_missing_value

WITH RECURSIVE date_range AS (
  SELECT DATE('2010-01-01') AS date
  UNION ALL
  SELECT DATE_ADD(date, INTERVAL 1 DAY)
  FROM date_range
  WHERE date < '2014-12-31'
),
holidays AS (
  SELECT date
  FROM date_range
  WHERE 
    -- Weekends
    DAYOFWEEK(date) IN (1, 7)
    -- New Year's Day (January 1)
    OR (MONTH(date) = 1 AND DAY(date) = 1)
    -- Independence Day (July 4)
    OR (MONTH(date) = 7 AND DAY(date) = 4)
    -- Veterans Day (November 11)
    OR (MONTH(date) = 11 AND DAY(date) = 11)
    -- Christmas Day (December 25)
    OR (MONTH(date) = 12 AND DAY(date) = 25)
),
user_holidays AS (
  SELECT 
    u.Id,
    COUNT(h.date) AS holiday_count
  FROM users u
  CROSS JOIN holidays h
  WHERE h.date BETWEEN 
    GREATEST(u.CreationDate, '2010-01-01') AND 
    LEAST(u.LastAccessDate, '2014-12-31')
  GROUP BY u.Id
)
SELECT
  u.Id,
  u.DisplayName,
  u.Age,
  DATEDIFF(
    LEAST(u.LastAccessDate, '2014-12-31'),
    GREATEST(u.CreationDate, '2010-01-01')
  ) + 1 - COALESCE(uh.holiday_count, 0) AS TotalDays
FROM users u
LEFT JOIN user_holidays uh ON u.Id = uh.Id
WHERE u.CreationDate <= '2014-12-31'
  AND u.LastAccessDate >= '2010-01-01'
ORDER BY u.Id

WITH DAILY_REVENUE AS (
    SELECT 
        Date, 
        SUM(Amount) AS TotalAmount 
    FROM transactions_1k 
    GROUP BY Date
),
MOVING_AVG AS (
    SELECT 
        Date,
        CASE 
            WHEN COUNT(*) OVER(ORDER BY Date ASC ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) = 3
            THEN SUM(TotalAmount) OVER(ORDER BY Date ASC ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)
            ELSE NULL 
        END AS TotalAmount,
        CASE 
            WHEN COUNT(*) OVER(ORDER BY Date ASC ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) = 3
            THEN CAST(AVG(TotalAmount) OVER(ORDER BY Date ASC ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS DECIMAL(10,2))
            ELSE NULL 
        END AS AverageAmount
    FROM DAILY_REVENUE
)
SELECT * FROM MOVING_AVG
ORDER BY Date ASC

WITH RECURSIVE set_hierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: start with all sets
    SELECT 
        s.id,
        s.code,
        s.parentCode,
        s.code AS ancestralCode,
        1 AS level
    FROM sets s
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: join with parent sets
    SELECT 
        sh.id,
        sh.code,
        p.parentCode,
        CONCAT(sh.ancestralCode, '-', p.code) AS ancestralCode,
        sh.level + 1 AS level
    FROM set_hierarchy sh
    INNER JOIN sets p ON sh.parentCode = p.code
)
SELECT 
    id,
    code,
    ancestralCode
FROM (
    SELECT 
        id,
        code,
        ancestralCode,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY code ORDER BY level DESC) AS rn
    FROM set_hierarchy
) ranked
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY code

SELECT 
    e.event_id,
    e.event_name,
    att.largest_attendance_member,
    exp.largest_expense_member,
    exp.avg_expense_cost,
    inc.largest_income_member,
    inc.avg_income_amount
FROM event e
LEFT JOIN (
    -- Get the largest member ID who attended this event
    SELECT 
        link_to_event,
        MAX(link_to_member) AS largest_attendance_member
    FROM attendance
    WHERE link_to_event = 'recI43CzsZ0Q625ma'
    GROUP BY link_to_event
) att ON e.event_id = att.link_to_event
LEFT JOIN (
    -- Get the largest member ID who had expenses for this event
    -- and calculate their average expense cost
    SELECT 
        b.link_to_event,
        MAX(exp.link_to_member) AS largest_expense_member,
        AVG(CASE WHEN exp.link_to_member = (
            SELECT MAX(e2.link_to_member)
            FROM expense e2
            JOIN budget b2 ON e2.link_to_budget = b2.budget_id
            WHERE b2.link_to_event = 'recI43CzsZ0Q625ma'
        ) THEN exp.cost END) AS avg_expense_cost
    FROM expense exp
    JOIN budget b ON exp.link_to_budget = b.budget_id
    WHERE b.link_to_event = 'recI43CzsZ0Q625ma'
    GROUP BY b.link_to_event
) exp ON e.event_id = exp.link_to_event
LEFT JOIN (
    -- Get the largest member ID who contributed income
    -- and calculate their average income amount
    SELECT 
        MAX(link_to_member) AS largest_income_member,
        AVG(CASE WHEN link_to_member = (
            SELECT MAX(link_to_member) FROM income
        ) THEN amount END) AS avg_income_amount
    FROM income
) inc ON 1=1
WHERE e.event_id = 'recI43CzsZ0Q625ma'

WITH RankedScores AS (
    SELECT 
        results.Segment,
        results.score as result_score,
        scores.score as available_score,
        scores.value,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY results.Segment, results.score 
            ORDER BY scores.score DESC
        ) as rn
    FROM customer_results results
    JOIN customer_scores scores 
        ON scores.Segment = results.Segment
        AND scores.score <= results.score
)
SELECT 
    Segment,
    result_score as score,
    value
FROM RankedScores
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY Segment, result_score

WITH Partitioned AS (
    SELECT *, 
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY CustomerID ORDER BY Date, Time) AS RowNumber 
    FROM transactions_1k
)
SELECT *, 
       CASE 
           WHEN RowNumber = 1 THEN 0 
           ELSE COALESCE(
               DATEDIFF(
                   a.Date, 
                   (SELECT MAX(Date) 
                    FROM transactions_1k 
                    WHERE Date < a.Date AND CustomerID = a.CustomerID)
               ), 
               0
           ) 
       END AS Days_between
FROM Partitioned a

SELECT DISTINCT hp1.hero_id as id1, hp2.hero_id as id2
FROM hero_power hp1
INNER JOIN hero_power hp2 ON hp1.hero_id < hp2.hero_id
GROUP BY hp1.hero_id, hp2.hero_id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT hp1.power_id) = COUNT(DISTINCT hp2.power_id)
   AND COUNT(DISTINCT hp1.power_id) = (
       SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT power_id) 
       FROM hero_power 
       WHERE hero_id = hp1.hero_id
   )
   AND COUNT(DISTINCT hp1.power_id) = (
       SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT power_id) 
       FROM hero_power 
       WHERE hero_id = hp2.hero_id
   )
   AND SUM(CASE WHEN hp1.power_id = hp2.power_id THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = COUNT(DISTINCT hp1.power_id)
ORDER BY id1, id2

WITH max_cost AS (
    SELECT MAX(cost) as highest_cost
    FROM expense
),
dept_expense_counts AS (
    SELECT m.department, COUNT(e.expense_id) as expense_count
    FROM expense e
    INNER JOIN member mem ON e.link_to_member = mem.member_id
    INNER JOIN major m ON mem.link_to_major = m.major_id
    CROSS JOIN max_cost mc
    WHERE e.cost = mc.highest_cost
    GROUP BY m.department
)
SELECT department, expense_count
FROM dept_expense_counts
WHERE expense_count = (SELECT MAX(expense_count) FROM dept_expense_counts)
ORDER BY department

SELECT 
    link_to_event,
    CASE 
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 THEN 'Closed'
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 
             AND MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 THEN 'Open'
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 THEN 'Open'
        ELSE 'Planning'
    END AS event_status,
    CASE 
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 
            THEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN remaining ELSE NULL END)
        WHEN MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 
             AND MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 
            THEN MAX(spent)
        ELSE MAX(remaining)
    END AS remaining_amount,
    SUM(amount) AS total_amount,
    SUM(spent) AS total_spent
FROM budget
WHERE link_to_event IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY link_to_event

    FROM
        schools AS s
    INNER JOIN
        satscores AS sa ON s.CDSCode = sa.cds
    WHERE
        s.County = 'Alameda'
        AND sa.rtype = 'S'
        AND sa.AvgScrRead IS NOT NULL

WITH ChangeHistory AS (
    SELECT
        s.CDSCode,
        s.School,
        s.FundingType,
        s.LastUpdate,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY s.CDSCode ORDER BY s.LastUpdate) AS ChangeSequence,
        LAG(s.School) OVER (PARTITION BY s.CDSCode ORDER BY s.LastUpdate) AS PreviousSchool,
        LAG(s.FundingType) OVER (PARTITION BY s.CDSCode ORDER BY s.LastUpdate) AS PreviousFundingType
    FROM schools s
),
ChangesOnly AS (
    SELECT
        CDSCode,
        School,
        FundingType,
        PreviousSchool,
        PreviousFundingType,
        ChangeSequence
    FROM ChangeHistory
    WHERE (PreviousSchool IS NOT NULL AND PreviousSchool <> School)
       OR (PreviousFundingType IS NOT NULL AND PreviousFundingType <> FundingType)
)
SELECT
    CDSCode,
    'School' AS ColumnName,
    ISNULL(PreviousSchool, '') AS OldValue,
    ISNULL(School, '') AS NewValue
FROM ChangesOnly
WHERE PreviousSchool IS NOT NULL AND PreviousSchool <> School

UNION ALL

SELECT
    CDSCode,
    'FundingType' AS ColumnName,
    ISNULL(PreviousFundingType, '') AS OldValue,
    ISNULL(FundingType, '') AS NewValue
FROM ChangesOnly
WHERE PreviousFundingType IS NOT NULL AND PreviousFundingType <> FundingType

ORDER BY
    CDSCode,
    ColumnName

    (9999, 3, 'POPLATEK TYDNE', '2024-10-22')
) AS source (account_id, district_id, frequency, date)
ON target.account_id = source.account_id
WHEN MATCHED THEN

        frequency = source.frequency,

WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN

    VALUES (source.account_id, source.district_id, source.frequency, source.date)

        e.event_name,

    FROM attendance a1

    JOIN event e ON a1.link_to_event = e.event_id
)

DECLARE @batch_size INT = 100;
DECLARE @rows_affected INT = 1;

WHILE @rows_affected > 0
BEGIN
    BEGIN TRANSACTION;
    
    -- Insert only records that don't already exist in player_stats
    INSERT INTO player_stats (player_id, match_id, goals, assists, yellow_cards, red_cards, minutes_played)
    SELECT TOP (@batch_size) 
        pst.player_id, 
        pst.match_id, 
        pst.goals, 
        pst.assists, 
        pst.yellow_cards, 
        pst.red_cards, 
        pst.minutes_played
    FROM player_stats_temp pst
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 
        FROM player_stats ps 
        WHERE ps.player_id = pst.player_id
    );
    
    SET @rows_affected = @@ROWCOUNT;
    
    -- Optional: Remove processed records from temp table to avoid reprocessing
    IF @rows_affected > 0
    BEGIN
        DELETE FROM player_stats_temp
        WHERE player_id IN (
            SELECT player_id 
            FROM player_stats
        );
    END
    
    COMMIT TRANSACTION;
    
    -- Small delay to reduce contention (optional)
    WAITFOR DELAY '00:00:00.100';
END

WITH OrderedContracts AS (
    -- Order contracts and get the previous end date for the same driver and contract type
    SELECT 
        DriverId,
        ContractType,
        StartDate,
        EndDate,
        LAG(EndDate) OVER (PARTITION BY DriverId, ContractType ORDER BY StartDate, EndDate) AS PrevEndDate
    FROM driverContracts
),
IslandIdentifier AS (
    -- Identify the start of a new "island" (non-overlapping group)
    -- A new island starts when the current StartDate is after the previous EndDate
    SELECT 
        DriverId,
        ContractType,
        StartDate,
        EndDate,
        CASE 
            WHEN PrevEndDate IS NULL THEN 1
            WHEN StartDate > DATEADD(DAY, 1, PrevEndDate) THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END AS IsNewIsland
    FROM OrderedContracts
),
IslandGroups AS (
    -- Create island group numbers using cumulative sum
    SELECT 
        DriverId,
        ContractType,
        StartDate,
        EndDate,
        SUM(IsNewIsland) OVER (PARTITION BY DriverId, ContractType ORDER BY StartDate, EndDate) AS IslandGroup
    FROM IslandIdentifier
)
-- Merge overlapping/consecutive contracts within each island
SELECT 
    DriverId,
    ContractType,
    MIN(StartDate) AS StartDate,
    MAX(EndDate) AS EndDate
FROM IslandGroups
GROUP BY DriverId, ContractType, IslandGroup
ORDER BY DriverId, ContractType, MIN(StartDate)
